scenario: Helix Marketplace, a retailer running its catalog on Camunda 8.8, packages a custom inbound connector module that subscribes to 12000 webhook events per day from upstream merchants. The team wants the connector class auto-discovered by the runtime without listing it in any element template attribute or annotation parameter across all 30 instances of its Self-Managed cluster.
question: Which file in the inbound connector module exposes the InboundConnectorExecutable implementation to the runtime?

options.text: A META-INF/MANIFEST.MF entry that lists the fully qualified executable class name under a Connector-Class attribute inside the connector JAR
A resources/META-INF/services/io.camunda.connector.api.inbound.InboundConnectorExecutable file listing the fully qualified executable class name
A connector-runtime.properties file that lists the inbound connector classes under a key named inbound.executable.classes inside the JAR
An element-templates/inbound-template-connector.json entry that exposes the fully qualified Java class under a runtimeClass attribute

a.text: Incorrect. The connector SDK does not rely on a Connector-Class manifest attribute. Manifest entries are not part of the documented discovery path for inbound connector executables in the SDK guide.

b.text: Correct. The SDK guide shows the resources/META-INF/services/io.camunda.connector.api.inbound.InboundConnectorExecutable file as the Service Provider Interface entry that makes the executable class discoverable by the connector runtime.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no connector-runtime.properties discovery file in the SDK; the runtime relies on the ServiceLoader SPI under META-INF/services rather than a properties key.

d.text: Incorrect. Element templates describe modeling building blocks and bindings; the runtime does not load the executable Java class from a runtimeClass attribute on the JSON element template.

explanation: Custom inbound connectors are made detectable through the standard Java ServiceLoader SPI. The connector project provides a file under resources/META-INF/services named after the InboundConnectorExecutable interface; the file lists the fully qualified implementation class so the runtime can load it at startup.
